weather.summary: Saturday: 9°C, broken clouds. Sunday: 11°C, scattered clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is shaping up to be fantastic for outdoor adventures! I've found some wonderful events close to home in Hackney, from museum trails to interactive theatre. Plus, there are some exciting options to book ahead for the Easter holidays.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed and informal setting to enjoy books with your toddler.

transport.fromHackney: Buses and Overground to Hackney Downs
transport.tip: Check term times before you go!

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Listen, play and make some noise, informal session.
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory and interactive, perfect for toddlers to explore sounds.

transport.tip: Arrive early as it's first-come, first-served.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: A 'doing' museum where kids can build skills and foster creative confidence in a playful environment. Interactive spaces and activities include sensory playscapes, a finger skateboard park, an 'Imagination Playground' construction zone, a performance and story-telling stage, and an open design studio.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed specifically for children with sensory play, soft spaces, and big blocks. Encourages curiosity, promotes creativity, and develops design thinking.

transport.tip: Avoid peak hours as it can get crowded.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Step into historical homes through the ages with hands-on exhibits and a beautiful enclosed garden.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits and a garden to explore, perfect for curious toddlers.

transport.tip: Pick up an activity sheet or explorer backpack from the front desk.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A hidden gem near Brick Lane with a community feel and lots of animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, community feel, and engaging Easter activities.

transport.fromHackney: Buses and Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then walk
transport.tip: Check out their Easter activities!

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: An interactive story centre with indoor and outdoor play spaces designed to inspire a love of reading and creativity.
activities.whyItWorks: Imaginative play areas and storytelling sessions cater to young children.

transport.tip: Check website for specific events and times.

activities.title: Planet Play at Chickenshed
activities.description: Interactive, sensory show.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for the very young, engaging all senses.

transport.tip: Check website for ticket availability and specific show details.

activities.title: The Line
activities.venue: Various locations between Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park and The O2
activities.description: London's free art trail that runs more than two miles between Queen Elizabeth Park and The O2, taking in things like Anish Kapoor's Orbit and Abigail Fallis' shopping trolley sculpture in the shape of a double helix.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines art with an outdoor walk, providing a change of scenery and opportunities for discussion.

transport.fromHackney: Various transport options depending on start and end points.
transport.tip: Plan your route in advance.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, volcanoes, and mammals in this iconic museum. Don't miss the earthquake simulator and Andy's clock from the CBeebies shows.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, interactive exhibits, and spacious galleries appeal to toddlers.

transport.tip: Download the museum map to guide your visit.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore space travel, experiments, technology and transport. Check out the free exhibits including iconic objects created over the past 250 years.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and dedicated STEM play areas for small children.

transport.tip: Ideal for kids with an interest in space, technology or transport.

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.description: Discover art and design from around the world with free activities for families every day. During the school holidays there are hands-on making events and performances to inspire the whole family.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on family events and performances during school holidays.

transport.tip: Activities are aimed at children aged 5-12 years old, but many can be enjoyed by under 5s.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore the Natural History Gallery, Aquarium, and Animal Walk. Enjoy craft workshops, storytelling sessions, and animal encounters.
activities.whyItWorks: Animal encounters, gardens, and interactive displays make it engaging for young children.

transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops on their website.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Included with entry to the ship
activities.description: Cutty Sark's chickens have gotten loose and laid eggs around the ship - can you spot all the hidden eggs? Follow the trail to spot hidden eggs around the ship and collect all 11 letters on each egg to unscramble the secret nautical code
activities.whyItWorks: Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's history and epic voyages. Once you've cracked the code, head to the Cutty Sark café under the hull to claim your chocolatey treat

transport.tip: Most suitable for young explorers age five+ and their families.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Fun and educational free museum with child-friendly activities, especially the All Hands Children's Gallery. On Saturdays, they have “Character Encounters”.
activities.whyItWorks: Role-play space for kids under 8 and lots of hands-on things for them to do.

transport.tip: Check out the All Hands Children's Gallery.

activities.title: Richmond Park
activities.description: The boost of rural adventure city families need, complete with herds of deer and thousands of acres to roam
activities.whyItWorks: Great open space for children to run around and spot wildlife.

transport.tip: Wrap up warm as it can get breezy.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: A fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail perfect for young explorers.

transport.fromHackney: District Line or Overground to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for the Easter period.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Combine animal spotting with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.tip: Pick up a trail card and crack the secret password.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with the excitement of a chocolate reward.

transport.tip: Dress for the weather as it's an outdoor activity.

activities.title: Playing Shakespeare with Deutsche Bank: Romeo and Juliet at Shakespeare's Globe
activities.description: Critically acclaimed 90-min version created especially for young people. (Plus Romeo & Juliet Family Workshop (9-11) 28 Mar-11 Apr, £10.)
activities.whyItWorks: Accessible introduction to Shakespeare for young audiences.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to London Bridge or Blackfriars
transport.tip: Consider the family workshop for a more interactive experience.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at the Southbank Centre
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families including shows, musical adventures, puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break. Also worth visiting is the REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground – a play space made out of repurposed materials (£7.50).
activities.whyItWorks: Mix of paid and free activities, appealing to different interests.

transport.tip: Check the schedule in advance and book tickets.

activities.title: Disney on Ice: Into the Magic
activities.description: Be inspired by Disney's Magical characters.
activities.whyItWorks: Disney's Magical characters will inspire all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee and Metropolitan Lines to Wembley Park
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for the Easter period.

activities.title: British Library – Fairy Tales
activities.description: Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, brought to life through books, theatre costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: A family-friendly event with multiple activities

transport.tip: Book in advance to secure tickets
